perturbation.original_text: 2. Non -Disclosure. Company will not make copies of , disclose, discuss, publish or dissemi nate Confidential Information any third person or entity. Company will not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than responding to the RFP.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non -Disclosure. Company will not make copies of , disclose, discuss, publish or dissemi nate Confidential Information any third person or entity, except as required by law. Company will use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and potentially responding to the RFP, and for no other purpose, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The clause 'except as required by law' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text strictly forbids disclosure, but the changed text allows it if legally mandated. This contradicts the overall confidentiality agreement, creating uncertainty about what constitutes a breach and when disclosure is permitted. The added phrase 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties' adds further contradiction, introducing the possibility that ad-hoc agreements can override the NDA's core principle of non-disclosure, conflicting directly with the original non-disclosure clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non -Disclosure. Company will not make copies of , disclose, discuss, publish or dissemi nate Confidential Information any third person or entity, except as required by law. Company will use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and potentially responding to the RFP, and for no other purpose, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties. Company will take reasonable precautions to secure and prevent any unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or disseminatio n of Confidential Information. Company may not at any time directly or indirectly communicate with the media in relation to the Confidential Information or any information with respect to the RFP without first obtaining the written permission of FNHA. Comp any acknowledges and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting it any rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information. Company further agrees to return Confidential Information to FNHA, if FNHA so direc ts, or to destroy Confidential Information once the RFP process completes or earlier of FNHA so directs. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and FNHA staff or with any other persons identified b y FNHA as having undertaken this Agreement. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons , if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and c onditions that FNHA may direct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2 introduces exceptions to the non-disclosure obligation ('except as required by law' and 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties'). The contradicted text in the provided excerpt is the changed text, but is still contradictory as it now allows for potential disclosures that were originally strictly forbidden, creating a direct conflict within the same clause. The additions create uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality, which introduces a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company further agrees to return Confidential Information to FNHA, if FNHA so direc ts, or to destroy Confidential Information once the RFP process completes or earlier of FNHA so directs.
perturbation.changed_text: Company further agrees to return Confidential Information to FNHA, if FNHA so direc ts, unless retention is required for regulatory compliance. Company may retain the information for archival purposes following the RFP completion if destroyed copies are unavailable.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that Confidential Information must be returned or destroyed, UNLESS retention is needed for regulatory compliance. This creates ambiguity as to when the company is actually required to return/destroy the information and opens the door for potential disputes over whether regulatory compliance actually requires retention. Furthermore, the added allowance for archival retention (if destroyed copies are unavailable) undermines the original requirement for destruction, suggesting an alternative, less secure retention option. This conflicts with the spirit of the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non -Disclosure. Company will not make copies of , disclose, discuss, publish or dissemi nate Confidential Information any third person or entity, except as required by law. Company will use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and potentially responding to the RFP, and for no other purpose, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties. Company will take reasonable precautions to secure and prevent any unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or disseminatio n of Confidential Information. Company may not at any time directly or indirectly communicate with the media in relation to the Confidential Information or any information with respect to the RFP without first obtaining the written permission of FNHA. Comp any acknowledges and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting it any rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information. Company further agrees to return Confidential Information to FNHA, if FNHA so direc ts, unless retention is required for regulatory compliance. Company may retain the information for archival purposes following the RFP completion if destroyed copies are unavailable. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and FNHA staff or with any other persons identified b y FNHA as having undertaken this Agreement. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons , if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and c onditions that FNHA may direct.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original requirement of returning or destroying Confidential Information (unless retention is required for regulatory compliance or for archival purposes). The Contradicted Text, which includes the changed text, now contains conflicting statements. It states the company will return or destroy the information, but then allows for retention under specific conditions. This creates a direct conflict because it is unclear when the company is obligated to return/destroy, and when it is permitted to retain the information. The added allowance for archival retention further undermines the original requirement for destruction, making the obligation ambiguous. This is an inconsistency that leads to in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons , if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and c onditions that FNHA may direct.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons, if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and conditions that FNHA may direct. However, any such permitted discussion must be documented and a record of the discussion provided to FNHA within 24 hours.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence 'However, any such permitted discussion must be documented and a record of the discussion provided to FNHA within 24 hours' introduces a new requirement that was not previously present. This requirement to document discussions and provide records to FNHA within 24 hours contradicts the earlier statement that discussions are permitted 'on the terms and conditions that FNHA may direct.' The original text allows for flexibility in how discussions are conducted, but the added sentence imposes a strict documentation requirement, limiting the freedom initially granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non -Disclosure. Company will not make copies of , disclose, discuss, publish or dissemi nate Confidential Information any third person or entity, except as required by law. Company will use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and potentially responding to the RFP, and for no other purpose, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing by both parties. Company will take reasonable precautions to secure and prevent any unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or disseminatio n of Confidential Information. Company may not at any time directly or indirectly communicate with the media in relation to the Confidential Information or any information with respect to the RFP without first obtaining the written permission of FNHA. Comp any acknowledges and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting it any rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information. Company further agrees to return Confidential Information to FNHA, if FNHA so direc ts, unless retention is required for regulatory compliance. Company may retain the information for archival purposes following the RFP completion if destroyed copies are unavailable. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and FNHA staff or with any other persons identified b y FNHA as having undertaken this Agreement. This Agreement does not preclude discussions of Confidential Information between the undersigned and other persons, if expressly permitted by staff of FNHA and those discussions are conducted on the terms and conditions that FNHA may direct. However, any such permitted discussion must be documented and a record of the discussion provided to FNHA within 24 hours.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the changed text introduces a mandatory documentation requirement for discussions, which conflicts with the original agreement that such discussions are allowed 'on the terms and conditions that FNHA may direct.' The contradicted text section contains both the original clause and the changed clause, which creates a contradiction because it imposes a stricter condition (documentation) than what was initially agreed upon in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to obtain an immediate injunction enjoining any breach of this Agreement, as well as the rig ht to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to obtain an immediate injunction enjoining any material breach of this Agreement, as well as the rig ht to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach. A 'material breach' is defined as a breach that causes demonstrable financial loss to FNHA.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces the concept of a 'material breach' and defines it as one that causes 'demonstrable financial loss to FNHA'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the initial statement that ANY unauthorized disclosure or use would cause irreparable harm and significant injury. Now, only breaches causing demonstrable financial loss are considered 'material' and subject to immediate injunction. This contradicts the idea that all breaches cause irreparable harm, as some breaches might not result in demonstrable financial loss, and therefore would not be subject to immediate injunction, weakening FNHA's protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Equitable Relief. Company acknowledges that all of the Confidential Information is owned solely by FNHA and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, Company agrees that FNHA will have the right to obtain an immediate injunction enjoining any material breach of this Agreement, as well as the rig ht to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law o r in equity for such a breach. A 'material breach' is defined as a breach that causes demonstrable financial loss to FNHA.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that *any* unauthorized disclosure causes irreparable harm and allows for an immediate injunction. The changed text introduces the concept of a 'material breach' defined as causing 'demonstrable financial loss', which now limits the application of immediate injunctions to only breaches meeting this new, narrower definition. This directly contradicts the initial assertion that *any* breach warrants such action because some breaches, while still unauthorized disclosures, might not cause demonstrable financial loss, thus not triggering the immediate injunction. This creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is also the same as the changed text, but the explanation shows how it is contradictory with the original, unmodified meaning.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information. This Agreement may not be amended except by the written agreement signed by authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information. This Agreement may be amended by the written agreement signed by authorized representatives of both parties or through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives indicating mutual agreement to the amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates amendments to be done soley by written agreement signed by authorized parties. The change allows for amendment through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives. This creates an in-text contradiction as it introduces an alternative method for amendment that wasn't originally permitted. The agreement now contains contradictory statements regarding how amendments can occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information. This Agreement may be amended by the written agreement signed by authorized representatives of both parties or through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives indicating mutual agreement to the amendment. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance w ith the laws of the Province of British Columbia , without regard to or application of choice -of-law rules or principles and the parties submit to the jurisdiction of BC courts. Company may not assign this Agreement or transfer any benefits hereunder, dire ctly or indirectly (through acquisition, merger or otherwise), and any attempt to do so will be void without the prior written consent of FNHA . The relationship of the parties is that of independent contractors, and not of agency, partners or the like.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments can only be made through written agreement signed by authorized representatives. The changed text introduces an alternative method of amendment - documented email correspondence between authorized representatives indicating mutual agreement. Since the contradicted text is identical to the changed text (as the location is the same), the change does indeed create a contradiction within the document, as it now contains two potentially conflicting methods for amending the agreement. Thus, the original text that stipulates one method, and the changed text allows another, this creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company may not assign this Agreement or transfer any benefits hereunder, dire ctly or indirectly (through acquisition, merger or otherwise), and any attempt to do so will be void without the prior written consent of FNHA .
perturbation.changed_text: Company may not assign this Agreement or transfer any benefits hereunder, dire ctly or indirectly (through acquisition, merger or otherwise), except to a wholly-owned subsidiary, and any attempt to do so without the prior written consent of FNHA will be void.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'except to a wholly-owned subsidiary' introduces an exception to the original rule prohibiting assignment or transfer without prior written consent of FNHA. Now, a transfer to a wholly-owned subsidiary is allowed without consent, which contradicts the original statement that *any* attempt to assign or transfer without consent will be void. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding under what circumstances the agreement can be assigned or benefits transferred.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the Confidential Information and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements concerning such Confidential Information. This Agreement may be amended by the written agreement signed by authorized representatives of both parties or through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives indicating mutual agreement to the amendment. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance w ith the laws of the Province of British Columbia , without regard to or application of choice -of-law rules or principles and the parties submit to the jurisdiction of BC courts. Company may not assign this Agreement or transfer any benefits hereunder, dire ctly or indirectly (through acquisition, merger or otherwise), except to a wholly-owned subsidiary, and any attempt to do so without the prior written consent of FNHA will be void. The relationship of the parties is that of independent contractors, and not of agency, partners or the like.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'any attempt' to assign or transfer without FNHA's consent is void. The changed text introduces an exception: transfer to a wholly-owned subsidiary is now allowed without consent. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text and includes the contradictory statement 'Company may not assign this Agreement or transfer any benefits hereunder...except to a wholly-owned subsidiary...' This exception directly contradicts the initial 'any attempt' clause, creating a clear in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
